01_initial_request.input_data: glycosylation data from regulatory submissions for FDA-approved therapeutic antibodies
01_initial_request.user_intent: establish a benchmark glycan profile for therapeutic antibody development, quality control, and regulatory compliance
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: benchmark glycan profile comprising nine prevalent glycan epitopes and their respective abundances

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Therapeutic monoclonal antibodies require glycosylation characterization for quality control. [Gap] No comprehensive benchmark glycan profile existed for FDA-approved mAbs. [Decision] To achieve benchmark establishment, chose to use Microsoft Excel to perform data compilation on 157 FDA-approved therapeutic antibody products, expecting to obtain organized product list with basic information.

parameters.data_source: FDA Office of Biotechnology Products (OBP) internal product list tracking Biologic License Applications (BLAs)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Compiled list of 157 therapeutic recombinant antibody products with FDA approval up to May 2023, including antibody type, expression system, and IgG subclass information.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Product list compiled with basic information. [Gap] Detailed glycosylation specifications and N-glycan profiles needed from regulatory submissions. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive data collection, chose to use FDA Label database and Antibody Society database to perform information extraction on antibody characteristics, expecting to obtain expression system and IgG subclass data.

parameters.data_fields: antibody type, expression system, IgG subclass

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Extracted antibody type, expression system (CHO 76%, NS0 11%, Sp2/0 6%, E. coli 5%, Yeast 1%, YB2/0 1%), and IgG subclass distribution (IgG1 78%, IgG2 7%, IgG4 13%, hybrid 2%) from public databases.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Basic antibody information extracted from public databases. [Gap] Quantitative N-glycan profiles and release specifications needed from regulatory submissions. [Decision] To achieve glycosylation data collection, chose to use electronic common technical document (eCTD) to perform glycosylation data extraction on drug substance release specifications, expecting to obtain N-glycan profiles and characterization data.

parameters.data_section: drug substance release specifications and characterization section of BLAs
parameters.sample_type: individual reference standard lots or averages from multiple drug substance batches
parameters.exclusion_criteria: antibodies from other cell lines (n=10), coformulation products with multiple APIs (n=6), intentionally engineered glycan profiles (non-glycosylated n=5, afucosylated n=4, low fucosylated n=3)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Extracted quantitative N-glycan profiles from 119 eCTDs for antibodies produced by CHO (n=95), NS0 (n=16), and Sp2/0 (n=8) cell lines.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] N-glycan profiles extracted from 119 eCTDs. [Gap] Data needs to be organized by expression system for comparative analysis. [Decision] To achieve systematic comparison, chose to use manual categorization to perform data grouping on antibody products by expression system, expecting to obtain organized groups for analysis.

parameters.CHO_subgroups: typical IgG1 mAbs (n=57), IgG1 reference products and biosimilars (n=27), other types of IgG1s (n=10), non-IgG1 antibodies (n=24), IgG1 Fc-fusion proteins (n=4)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Products grouped by expression system with CHO group further divided into five subgroups based on antibody type and IgG subclass.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Data grouped by expression system. [Gap] Percentage values need standardization for consistent comparison. [Decision] To achieve data standardization, chose to use rounding rules to perform data processing on glycan percentage values, expecting to obtain standardized abundance values.

parameters.sorting_criteria: mean relative abundance within each antibody group

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Percentage values standardized with rounding rules applied; individual glycan species sorted based on mean relative abundance within each group.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Data processed and standardized. [Gap] Visual representation needed to compare glycan profiles across groups. [Decision] To achieve visual comparison, chose to use GraphPad Prism 9.5.1 to perform scatter plot generation on top ten most abundant glycans, expecting to obtain visual representation of glycan distribution.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated scatter plots illustrating mean values and distribution of top ten N-glycans for CHO-produced mAbs (Fig. 2), NS0-produced mAbs (Fig. 3A), and Sp2/0-produced mAbs (Fig. 3B).

references.citation: Neelamegham S, et al. Updates to the Symbol Nomenclature for Glycans guidelines. Glycobiology. 2019
references.relevance: Provides standardized glycan structure nomenclature used for drawing N-glycan structures

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Visual representations created for glycan profiles. [Gap] Prevalent glycan epitopes need to be identified across all groups. [Decision] To achieve benchmark establishment, chose to use comparative analysis to perform epitope identification on glycan structures across expression systems, expecting to obtain nine prevalent glycan epitopes with abundances.

parameters.analysis_scope: top ten glycans across all expression system groups

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified nine prevalent glycan epitopes: terminal N-acetylglucosamine, core fucose, terminal galactose, high mannose, α-galactose, terminal α2,3-linked N-acetylneuraminic acid, terminal α2,6-linked N-glycolylneuraminic acid, triantennary structure, and bisecting N-acetylglucosamine.

references.citation: Ghaderi D, et al. Implications of the presence of N-glycolylneuraminic acid in recombinant therapeutic glycoproteins. Nat Biotechnol. 2010
references.relevance: Supports identification of NGNA as potentially immunogenic epitope

references.citation: Ferrara C, et al. Unique carbohydrate-carbohydrate interactions are required for high affinity binding between FcgammaRIII and antibodies lacking core fucose. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A. 2011
references.relevance: Supports significance of core fucose epitope for ADCC effects

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparative analysis of glycosylation data from FDA regulatory submissions (eCTD documents) across 119 therapeutic antibodies produced by CHO, NS0, and Sp2/0 cell lines

total_antibodies_analyzed.interpretation: Number of FDA-approved therapeutic antibodies with quantitative N-glycan profiles analyzed from eCTD submissions

prevalent_epitopes_identified.interpretation: Nine prevalent glycan epitopes identified across all expression systems forming the benchmark profile

top_three_glycans_abundance_range.interpretation: Combined mean abundance of G0F, G1F, and G2F glycans across all groups falls within narrow range

CHO_cell_line_usage.interpretation: Percentage of FDA-approved therapeutic antibodies produced using CHO cell expression system

products_with_glycosylation_specificatio.interpretation: Percentage of products with glycosylation specifications for release testing of drug substance

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Objective achieved - A benchmark glycan profile comprising nine prevalent glycan epitopes (terminal N-acetylglucosamine, core fucose, terminal galactose, high mannose, α-galactose, terminal α2,3-linked N-acetylneuraminic acid, terminal α2,6-linked N-glycolylneuraminic acid, triantennary structure, and bisecting N-acetylglucosamine) with their respective abundances has been successfully established from analysis of 119 FDA-approved therapeutic antibodies, enabling assessment of glycosylation consistency for therapeutic antibody development, quality control, and regulatory compliance.
